zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Tunstall de Snede於1332年出生在英國英格蘭爾維拉亞斯頓時，他的父親Count Henry de Sneyde of  Tunstall and Stafford62歲，母親Margaret de Tunstall30歲。他和Idonia Aldithley Aldithley Audley至少有2個兒子。他去世於1390年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾彤絲朵 白 阿德巴斯頓享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas de Tunstall de Snede于1332年出生在英国英格兰爾維拉亞斯頓时，他的父亲Count Henry de Sneyde of  Tunstall and Stafford62岁，母亲Margaret de Tunstall30岁。他和Idonia Aldithley Aldithley Audley至少有2个儿子。他去世于1390年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾彤絲朵 白 阿德巴斯頓享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tunstall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): habitational name from any of various places called with Old English tūn-stall ‘site of a farm, farmstead’, notably Tunstall (Lancashire) and one or more of many Staffordshire places (Tunstall near Stoke on Trent, Tunstall near Adbaston, and four places called Dunstall), but also Tunstall Farm in Mountfield (Sussex) and possibly also Tunstall (Durham, Kent, Norfolk, Shropshire, Suffolk, east and North Yorkshire), and Dunstall (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Tunstill
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Tunstall  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Snede
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Sneyd in Burslem, The Sneyd near Bloxwich (both Staffordshire), or Snead Farm and Snead Common in Rock (Worcestershire), all derived from Middle English snede ‘detached piece of land or woodland’ (Old English snǣd).

variants.name: Sneath
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and Rutland): habitational name from Snaith (Yorkshire), from Old Norse sneith ‘slice of land, piece of land cut off’. Compare Sneed .

variants.name: Snead
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Sneed  .

variants.name: Snee
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Sneed   with loss of the final consonant.
Irish (Mayo and Sligo): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sniadhaigh ‘descendant of Sniadhach’, a personal name apparently meaning ‘nitty’, from sneadh ‘nit’.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Tunstall de Snede was born in 1332, in Willaston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Count Henry de Sneyde of  Tunstall and Stafford, was 62 and his mother, Margaret de Tunstall, was 30. He had at least 2 sons with Idonia Aldithley Aldithley Audley. He died in 1390, in Tunstall by Adbaston, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Idonia Aldithley Aldithley Audley. Il est décédé en 1390, à Tunstall by Adbaston, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans.
